agenda_pressure: Favor durable metatheorems over additional local slash identities
Keep separability-style characterizations active now that transport infrastructure exists
Prefer problems that clarify reducibility, admissibility, or exact boundaries over cosmetic symmetry completion
current_bottlenecks: No verified theorem packages the replacement lemmas into a general admissibility or reduction principle
No boundary result constrains how much derivability survives under atomic or otherwise restricted hypotheses
Current open composition lemmas look too local to improve the global theory summary on their own
desired_summary_changes: Turn contextual replacement into a clearer cut-like or substitution-style reducibility principle
Add a sharp boundary characterization for derivability in constrained regimes
Explain how left- and right-division transport interact in reusable higher-level proof patterns
missing_bridges: From contextual replacement theorems to an explicit general substitution or cut-like admissibility statement
From slash transport behavior to derivability criteria in restricted syntactic classes
From local application/composition facts to a unified account of structural reducibility

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize results that either sharpen the new context-transport backbone into clearer reducibility principles or add boundary characterizations for restricted derivability regimes.
next_direction.label: boundary_and_reduction_sharpening
next_direction.rationale: The core transport bridge is now verified on both slash sides, so the biggest remaining summary gains come from either compressing that bridge into stronger reusable reduction principles or pairing it with a sharp derivability boundary such as the atomic regime; there is no counterexample pressure forcing a narrower impossibility-focused direction yet.

overexplored_patterns: One-step slash composition lemmas that only combine nearby derived rules
Symmetric left/right closure variants without a new criterion or reduction mechanism
Problems whose payoff stays inside a single append-based proof path

canonical_targets: A deeper understanding of completeness for $L$
The boundary between cut elimination and decidability in extensions
soft_constraints: Closeness to established notation and theory
themes: Structural correspondences between language hierarchies and logical systems
Extraction of logical rules and complexity

theory_snapshot: The theory now has a genuine metatheoretic backbone: both left- and right-division judgments can be transported into larger ambient sequents through verified context-replacement theorems, and right division also has a direct append-application rule. The main frontier is no longer basic slash-to-context transport, but rather understanding what stronger reusable principles follow from these transport results and where their limits are. A secondary gap is the absence of any verified boundary theorem for restricted regimes such as atom-only contexts. There are still no verified counterexamples, so the summary is constrained more by missing characterizations than by known impossibility boundaries.
